title: 💰 Hiring Family in Your Business: Tax Rules 2026
description:
How US and UK self employed can legally hire family members in their business in 2026, the tax savings, the IRS audit risks, and the structures that work.

Personal finance, family employment, hiring spouse, hiring kids, freelancer taxes 2026.

segments.voice: Hiring family members in your business is one of the most powerful and least understood tax strategies for US self-employed people. Done correctly under IRS rules, it can save thousands annually. Done sloppily, it triggers audits and reclassification. Today, the rules that actually work, citing the IRS regulations directly.

Hiring your child under eighteen in a sole prop or single-member LLC. Per IRC section 3121(b)(3), wages paid to your own child under age eighteen by an unincorporated business are exempt from FICA, both Social Security and Medicare. The wages are also exempt from FUTA up to age twenty-one. So your kid pays no payroll tax and you pay no employer payroll tax.

Income tax angle for the kid. The two thousand twenty-six standard deduction is fifteen thousand dollars for single filers. So a child earning up to fifteen thousand pays zero federal income tax on the wages. The full fifteen thousand also moves out of your taxable income at your bracket. At a twenty-two percent bracket, that is thirty-three hundred dollars in real tax savings annually.

What real work qualifies. The IRS rejects fake jobs immediately during audit. Your child must perform actual work suitable for their age. Filing, basic bookkeeping, social media content creation, photography for the business, packing orders for an ecommerce business, light data entry, all qualify. The work must be documented with timesheets and a written job description.

Pay rate that holds up. The wage must reflect market rates for the work performed. A teenager doing basic data entry should earn fifteen to eighteen dollars an hour, not seventy-five. Reasonable compensation comparable to what you would pay a non-family employee for the same work survives audit. Inflated wages get reclassified.

Documentation requirements. Written job description, timesheets showing hours worked, copy of all checks or transfers, payroll records, W-4 form if pay exceeds the withholding threshold, year-end W-2 form. The IRS audit guide for family business arrangements specifically lists these documents in the Internal Revenue Manual.

Roth IRA contributions for kids with earned income. Once your kid has W-2 earned income from your business, they can contribute up to seven thousand dollars annually to a Roth IRA. Charles Schwab, Fidelity, and Vanguard all offer custodial Roth IRAs for minors. A kid contributing for ten years from age fourteen to twenty-three could have over one hundred thousand by age sixty just from compounding.

Real example. Mike runs a freelance photography business as a sole prop. He hires his thirteen-year-old daughter to do social media content creation for fifteen hours a week at fifteen dollars an hour, totaling around eleven thousand seven hundred dollars annually. The wage is below her standard deduction so she pays zero income tax. Mike deducts the wage on Schedule C, saving roughly twenty-five hundred at his bracket plus SE tax.

Hiring your spouse. Different rules apply. Spouses do not get the FICA exemption. So spouse wages are subject to full payroll tax, fifteen point three percent combined employer plus employee. The trade-off is the spouse can contribute to retirement accounts based on earned income, providing long-term tax-advantaged growth.

When hiring spouse makes sense. If your spouse is not otherwise working, paying them a salary from the business creates earned income that lets them contribute to a SEP-IRA, Solo 401(k), or traditional IRA. The retirement contributions are deductible against business income. The combined family tax savings often exceed the FICA cost on spouse wages.

Health Reimbursement Arrangement for spouse employees. If your spouse is a bona fide employee of the business, you can establish a Section 105 Health Reimbursement Arrangement that reimburses family medical expenses tax-free. The arrangement must follow specific rules and is simpler with a sole prop than other entities. The savings on out-of-pocket medical can be substantial.

Hiring grandparents and adult children. No FICA exemption for these relationships. Standard payroll tax rules apply. The advantage is shifting income to a lower-bracket family member, useful when your retired parents are at lower tax brackets. The math depends on the specific bracket spread, but income shifting works for any family hire.

S corporation considerations. The FICA exemption for children under eighteen does not apply if your business is structured as an S corporation. So if you hire your kid in an S corp, full FICA applies. This is one of the surprising disadvantages of S corp election that catches family business owners off guard.
segments.visual: S corp child FICA exemption loss

segments.voice: The fix for S corps with kids. Some families establish a separate sole proprietorship that employs the children, with that sole prop providing services to the S corp. The structure must have legitimate business purpose and arms-length pricing. CPAs experienced in family business taxation can structure this defensibly.

Common audit triggers in family employment. Wages disproportionate to age, time, and skill of family worker. No timesheets or job descriptions. Payments routed through personal accounts rather than business accounts. Family member attending school full-time and somehow logging forty hours per week. Each of these gets the deduction reclassified in audit.

Records audit will demand. Bank statements showing the wage payments from business account to family member. Timesheets. Job descriptions. W-2 or 1099 forms issued. Tax returns of the family member showing the wage was reported. Schedule C showing the deduction. Each of these documents must align with the others.

Payroll mechanics. Use a real payroll service even for one family employee. Gusto, OnPay, and Patriot Payroll handle this for around forty dollars a month. The service withholds appropriate taxes, files quarterly 941 forms, and issues W-2s at year-end. DIY payroll for a single family employee is technically legal but creates audit risk if anything is documented poorly.

529 college savings interaction. Wages paid to your kid that the kid then contributes to a Roth IRA come back to the family in the form of long-term retirement assets. Wages paid that the kid uses for spending money do not have the same compounding effect. The strategy of paying kids for real work and helping them save creates multi-decade benefits.

Education and legal expenses paid by parents do not count. The IRS does not allow you to call educational expenses for your kid wages. Tuition, school supplies, sports equipment, all classic personal expenses. Hiring your kid for real work for real wages, then letting them spend or save those wages, is a separate transaction.

Workers compensation for family employees. State law varies. Some states require workers comp coverage for any employee including family. Other states allow exemptions for sole proprietorships hiring immediate family. Check your state department of labor website for specific rules. Skipping required workers comp triggers separate state penalties beyond IRS issues.

Unemployment insurance interactions. Federal Unemployment Tax Act exempts wages paid by a parent to a child under age twenty-one. State unemployment insurance varies. Many states follow the federal exemption, others do not. Saving on unemployment tax requires verifying state-specific rules in addition to federal.

UK readers, hiring family in your business. The basic rules. Pay must be reasonable for the work performed. Payments must be processed through PAYE if salary exceeds the National Insurance threshold. HMRC scrutinizes family employment closely under their settlements legislation under section 624 of ITTOIA 2005, which can apply to redirect income back to the original earner.

UK Settlement Provisions trap. If you give shares in your limited company to a non-working spouse, the dividend income may be reattributed back to you under the Settlement Provisions, eliminating any tax savings. The Arctic Systems case from two thousand seven established important guidelines but the rules remain a real audit risk for incorrectly structured family arrangements.

Practical strategy. If you are sole prop or single-member LLC, hiring your kid under eighteen for real age-appropriate work at fair market wages is one of the cleanest family tax strategies. Document everything, run real payroll, and the IRS rarely challenges. The combined federal income tax and FICA savings make this one of the highest-return family tax strategies available.
